title: Validate every value crossing the user boundary
description: Treat all data from user space as hostile: copy it in with the accessor APIs, bounds-check sizes and indices, and never trust a user pointer.
body: Use when implementing a syscall, ioctl, or any kernel entry that takes user-supplied data. Do not dereference a user pointer directly and do not trust a user-provided length or index. Actions: copy scalars/buffers with copy_from_user/get_user, check the return for faults, validate sizes and indices against bounds before use, and re-validate after copy (no TOCTOU on shared user memory). Validation signals include checked accessor return values and fault-injection tests that pass an EFAULT/invalid pointer without crashing. Risk boundary: an unchecked user pointer or missing bounds check is a kernel-privilege exploit, not just a bug; treat any direct user-pointer dereference as a security-blocking defect and escalate for review.
